incident.title: Active Directory Full Kill Chain — LLMNR Poisoning to Golden Ticket
incident.outcome: Full domain compromise. All domain hashes exfiltrated. Golden Ticket forged. psexec blocked by Defender.

kill_chain.name: LLMNR Poisoning
kill_chain.result: NTLMv2 hash captured for Administrator from 10.10.10.40. Hash saved to /usr/share/responder/logs/SMB-NTLMv2-SSP-fe80::c1b1:46ee:a893:40ea.txt. Captured on IPv6 address.
kill_chain.detection_note: VIS-001 — intra-LAN traffic never routes through OPNsense/Suricata.

kill_chain.name: Hash Cracking
kill_chain.detection_note: Offline operation — no logging system can detect this.

kill_chain.name: WinRM Initial Access
kill_chain.result: Interactive shell on DC01 as CORP\Administrator.
kill_chain.detection_note: EC4624, NTLM, Logon_Type=3, src_ip=10.10.10.99. 12 events total.

kill_chain.name: Pass-the-Hash
kill_chain.result: Shell on DC01 using NT hash only — no plaintext password needed.
kill_chain.detection_note: EC4624, NTLM, src_ip=10.10.10.99. Indistinguishable from Stage 3 in logs.

kill_chain.name: DCSync
kill_chain.result: All domain hashes exfiltrated via DRSUAPI replication protocol.
kill_chain.detection_note: EC4662 with GUIDs 1131f6aa + 1131f6ad in _raw. 76 events. Must search _raw — Splunk truncates Properties field.

kill_chain.name: Golden Ticket
kill_chain.result: Forged TGT saved as Administrator.ccache. Valid until krbtgt password reset twice.
kill_chain.detection_note: EC4769 for Administrator with zero preceding EC4768. Service tickets with no TGT request = forged ticket.

kill_chain.name: psexec Attempt
kill_chain.result: Blocked by Windows Defender. Three attempts — OOwN, rnDL, QYag. Shell never obtained.
kill_chain.detection_note: EC7045, random 4-char service names, binary in %systemroot%. 4 events (3 impacket attempts).

splunk_evidence.detection: NTLM Admin logon from attacker IP
splunk_evidence.query: index=windows EventCode=4624 Account_Name=Administrator Authentication_Package=NTLM | table _time, Account_Name, src_ip, Logon_Type | sort -_time
index=windows EventCode=4662 | search _raw="*1131f6aa*" OR _raw="*1131f6ad*" | where NOT match(Account_Name, "\\$$")
splunk_evidence.result: 76 events — Administrator triggering both replication GUIDs

splunk_evidence.query: index=windows (EventCode=4768 OR EventCode=4769) Account_Name="Administrator@CORP.LOCAL" | table _time, EventCode, Service_Name
splunk_evidence.result: 8 x EC4769 — zero EC4768 for Administrator
4 events — OOwN, rnDL, QYag service names

detections.name: NTLM Admin logon from attacker IP
DCSync replication rights abuse
detections.filter: _raw contains 1131f6aa or 1131f6ad, Account_Name not ending in $

visibility_gaps.description: Suricata blind to all intra-LAN attacks
visibility_gaps.impact: LLMNR, WinRM, PtH, DCSync, Golden Ticket invisible at network layer
visibility_gaps.fix: vSwitch port mirroring — SPAN to OPNsense em1 (Sprint 4)

visibility_gaps.description: Sysmon not confirmed flowing to Splunk
visibility_gaps.impact: No network connection telemetry from DC01
visibility_gaps.fix: Verify Sysmon install and UF inputs.conf on dc-vm (Sprint 4)
Accepted — prevent hash theft upstream by disabling LLMNR

infrastructure_fixes.description: Custom regex to parse Source Network Address from Windows Security events
Enables EC4662 generation for DCSync detection
Changed facility local5 to local6 to separate EVE JSON from general syslog

recommendations: Disable LLMNR via GPO immediately — CorpDisableLLMNR GPO already exists, enable it
Reset krbtgt password twice with 10-hour gap to invalidate Golden Ticket
Enforce SMB signing on all hosts not just DCs
Install Sysmon on DC01 and verify forwarding to Splunk (close VIS-002)
Implement LAPS for local admin passwords
Enable Protected Users security group for privileged accounts
